audienceSections.normal: This chapter teaches [[grammar:ook al + subordinate clause]] and [[grammar:zestig tot honderd and compounds]]. Use [[grammar:ook al]] when the main event continues despite another fact. Learn sixty through one hundred and keep using [[grammar:unit + en + tens]] for compound numbers.
audienceSections.expert: This chapter introduces [[grammar:ook al + subordinate clause]]: [[grammar:Ook al]] introduces a concessive subordinate clause with verb-final order.; [[grammar:zestig tot honderd and compounds]]: Decade forms are lexical bases; compounds retain [[grammar:unit-before-ten]] order, while [[grammar:honderd]] is the hundred base.

audienceSections.normal:
#### ook al + subordinate clause

Use [[grammar:ook al]] when the main event continues despite another fact. Example: [[grammar:Ook al traint hij voor honderd kilometer, wij kiezen een kortere route.]]

#### zestig tot honderd and compounds

Learn sixty through one hundred and keep using [[grammar:unit + en + tens]] for compound numbers. Example: [[grammar:De tweede route is tweeënzeventig kilometer.]]
audienceSections.expert:
#### ook al + subordinate clause

[[grammar:Ook al]] introduces a concessive subordinate clause with verb-final order. Literal example: [[grammar:Ook al traint hij voor honderd kilometer, wij kiezen een kortere route.]]

#### zestig tot honderd and compounds

Decade forms are lexical bases; compounds retain [[grammar:unit-before-ten]] order, while [[grammar:honderd]] is the hundred base. Literal example: [[grammar:De tweede route is tweeënzeventig kilometer.]]

audienceSections.normal: [[grammar:Ook al]] introduces a concession. Dutch compound numbers continue to use [[grammar:unit + en + tens]] up to 99; [[grammar:honderd]] introduces 100.
audienceSections.expert: [[grammar:Ook al]] introduces a concession. Dutch compound numbers continue to use [[grammar:unit + en + tens]] up to 99; [[grammar:honderd]] introduces 100.

breakdown.normal:
- [[grammar:Welke route fietsen we zaterdag?]] — Which route are we cycling on Saturday?
- [[grammar:De korte route is zestig kilometer.]] — The short route is sixty kilometers.
- [[grammar:De tweede route is tweeënzeventig kilometer.]] — The second route is seventy-two kilometers.
- [[grammar:Ook al is die langer, hij gaat langs het water.]] — Although it is longer, it runs along the water.
- [[grammar:De derde route is tachtig kilometer.]] — The third route is eighty kilometers.
- [[grammar:Negentig kilometer is voor mij te ver.]] — Ninety kilometers is too far for me.
- [[grammar:Alex fietste vorig jaar negenennegentig kilometer.]] — Alex cycled ninety-nine kilometers last year.
- [[grammar:Ook al traint hij voor honderd kilometer, wij kiezen een kortere route.]] — Although he is training for one hundred kilometers, we choose a shorter route.
- [[grammar:Voor ons is zeventig kilometer genoeg.]] — For us, seventy kilometers is enough.
- [[grammar:Dan is de route van tweeënzeventig kilometer net te lang.]] — Then the seventy-two-kilometer route is just a little too long.
- [[grammar:Goed, we kiezen de route van zestig kilometer.]] — Good, we choose the sixty-kilometer route.
- [[grammar:We vertrekken vroeg, ook al regent het een beetje.]] — We will leave early even if it rains a little.
